message:
feat(ffs-fuse): add backpressure throttle tier, FUSE queue tuning, and mount timeout

Three improvements to the FUSE layer:

**Backpressure throttle tier:**

The backpressure gate previously had only two decisions: Proceed or Shed.
When the system was under moderate pressure (degraded but not critical),
write requests were immediately shed, causing unnecessary ENOSPC/EIO
errors. A new \`Throttle\` decision tier is inserted between Proceed and
Shed: the request is delayed by \`BACKPRESSURE_THROTTLE_DELAY\` (5ms)
before proceeding, giving the flush loop time to drain without dropping
the request. \`should_shed\` now handles all three variants.

**FUSE kernel queue tuning:**

When \`worker_threads\` is explicitly set (non-zero) in MountOptions,
the mount now passes \`max_background\` and \`congestion_threshold\`
FUSE mount options to the kernel. \`max_background\` is set to the
resolved thread count, and \`congestion_threshold\` to 75% of that
(minimum 1). This allows the kernel FUSE queue depth to match the
configured parallelism, reducing unnecessary blocking under load. The
MountOptions doc comment is updated to reflect this behavior.

**Mount handle wait timeout:**

\`MountHandle::wait()\` previously polled the shutdown flag in an
infinite loop, which meant a hung or unresponsive FUSE session would
block forever. The wait loop now respects \`config.unmount_timeout\`:
if the timeout elapses without a shutdown signal, it logs a warning
and proceeds to unmount. This prevents test hangs and allows graceful
recovery in production.

Tests added for all three features.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
